description: Structured DKG + threshold-signing behavioral vectors for the Pulsar proof boundary. Spec-level state vectors (positive + negative), each grounded in the exact v0.6.3 code constant that enforces it. Distinct from the byte-level wire KATs under vectors/{keygen,sign,verify,threshold-sign,dkg}.json (see cross_version_kats).

claim_discipline.A_verifier_compat: PROVEN: threshold (c-tilde,z,h) is byte-FIPS-204; unmodified circl mldsa{65,87}.Verify accepts. Proven in three settings: no-reconstruct BCC (bcc_sign_test.go:86), DEALERLESS RSS key (mithril_rss_test.go:38 all N<=6; mithril_rss_n8_test.go:16 T==N at n=8,n=16), and NO-RECONSTRUCT HYPERBALL over the RSS key (mithril_rss_hyperball_test.go:41). interop-tested.
claim_discipline.B_construction: SCOPED (Lux-authored). Dealerless committee keygen (RSS/Mithril) + no-reconstruct signing (BCC/CSCP + Mithril hyperball) + standard-ML-DSA-verifier output, malicious-hardened; the full FIPS-204 KeyGen-distribution-equivalence proof and the malicious-secure CSCP/identifiable-abort layer are labelled residuals.
claim_discipline.keygen_residual: v0.6 CLOSES the dealerless-keygen gap for Claim (A): MithrilRSSKeygen (mithril_rss.go:133) is DEALERLESS (no dealer; no party holds the whole (s1,s2); s2 stays small as ||s2||inf <= C(N,N-T+1)*eta), and its threshold signatures verify under stock circl (mithril_rss_test.go:70, mithril_rss_n8_test.go:40). The v0.6.3 accumulateSubset (mithril_rss.go:122) per-subset mod-q reduction fixes a uint32 overflow that wrapped the key once C>=512, unblocking large committees (e.g. n=16,t=14, C(16,3)=560). The naive-additive byte-FIPS DKG stays correctly fail-closed (ErrDealerlessByteFIPSUnreachable) — RSS is its named escape, not a weakening. WHAT REMAINS RESIDUAL: full FIPS-204 KeyGen-distribution-equivalence (unbiased composite secret, hiding vs <t, abort-bias) is open-research — structural facts + stock-circl-verifiability are proven, the distributional equivalence is not. SIGNING is no-reconstruct (BCC/CSCP and hyperball) + malicious-hardened; permissionless dealerless safety is additionally carried by the Corona leg.

positive_vectors.scenario: Run the DEALERLESS Mithril RSS DKG (no trusted dealer) for every viable committee, reconstruct from a T-quorum, BCC-sign, and verify under unmodified circl mldsa65.Verify.
positive_vectors.invariant: mldsa65.Verify(rss.pub, msg, ctx, sig)==true for all 15 committees 2<=T<=N<=6 and for T==N at n=8,n=16; tamper/wrong-message rejected
positive_vectors.property: claim-A verifier-compatibility (the gold proof of dealerless stock-FIPS-204 signability)

positive_vectors.scenario: For the whole viability range, assert no single party holds all subsets and no (T-1)-coalition covers all subsets.
positive_vectors.invariant: for all id: |holdings[id]| < C(N,N-T+1); for all (T-1)-coalitions: covered != full

positive_vectors.scenario: Sign the dealerless RSS key via the Mithril 3-round HYPERBALL protocol; NO party or coordinator forms the full s1/s2/y/w0/sk; the signing path never calls ReconstructKeyMaterial.
positive_vectors.invariant: mldsa65.Verify accepts; AST scan finds no ReconstructKeyMaterial call; public transcript contains no share s1_(j) nor full s1/s2; mask y never on the wire
positive_vectors.code_ref: mithril_rss_hyperball.go:780 (SignHyperball), :261 (newHyperballParty no-reconstruct boundary), :870 (partyShareS1 never sums across parties), :518 (finalize fail-closed VerifyCtx :613)
positive_vectors.test_ref: TestHyperballStockCirclVerify mithril_rss_hyperball_test.go:41; TestHyperballNoReconstructStructural :100 (AST :109, transcript oracle :149); TestHyperballMaskNeverOnWire :529
positive_vectors.property: claim-A verifier-compatibility; claim-B secrecy/no-reconstruct over the dealerless key

positive_vectors.scenario: No-reconstruct BCC/CEF threshold signature (single group key) verified under an independent circl public key re-derived from the seed (no pulsar code in the verify path).
positive_vectors.invariant: mldsa65.Verify(pk, msg, ctx, sig)==true; transcript carries no leaking secret

positive_vectors.scenario: Legacy seed-share lineage: reconstruct the master seed from a threshold share set, recompute KeyFromSeed(masterSeed), compare to the DKG GroupPubkey; then sign and FIPS-204-verify.
positive_vectors.invariant: GroupPK == KeyFromSeed(masterSeed).Pub AND Verify(GroupPK, msg, sig)==ok
positive_vectors.property: correctness of the legacy seed-share map (structural seed-equivalence; NOT KeyGen-distribution-equivalence; superseded by RSS for the dealerless claim)

negative_vectors.scenario: Attempt RSS keygen for a non-viable committee: structurally invalid (T<2 or T>N) or norm-blown (tau*C(N,N-T+1)*eta >= gamma2, e.g. n=16,t=12).
negative_vectors.expected_outcome: rss.ValidateCommittee error (ErrCommittee) — never a wall key
negative_vectors.code_ref: mithril_rss.go:134 (ValidateCommittee gate); rss.ValidateCommittee per-(N,T) norm bound
negative_vectors.property: keygen viability (per-(N,T) norm bound; admits n=8,t=7 48x margin and n=16,t=14 2.4x; rejects n=16,t=12)

negative_vectors.scenario: Hyperball sign with a below/above-threshold or malformed active set (too few, too many, unsorted, duplicate, out-of-range id).
A malicious member biases its partial z_{j,k} on every accepted slot (keeps Accepted=true to look live).
negative_vectors.expected_outcome: no slot yields a stock-verifiable signature (FindHint + fail-closed VerifyCtx release gate); blameSlot pinpoints the culprit WITHOUT forming Sum_j T_j = t - s2 (leak-free)
negative_vectors.code_ref: mithril_rss_hyperball.go:518 (finalize release gate), :638 (blameSlot leak-free), :633-637 (documented attribution residual)
negative_vectors.property: robustness (fail-closed release gate + leak-free identifiable abort)

negative_vectors.scenario: A rushing party reveals a DIFFERENT w for a slot in Round 2 than it committed in Round 1.
negative_vectors.expected_outcome: aggregateCommitments returns a w-commitment mismatch (equivocation) error
negative_vectors.code_ref: mithril_rss_hyperball.go:466 (binding check: revealed w must match CommitW)

negative_vectors.scenario: Assert the secret mask y_j (and the share s1_(j)) bytes never appear in the Round-2/Round-3 wire data.
negative_vectors.expected_outcome: wire carries only w_{j,k}=A*y_{j,k} (recovering y is Module-SIS) and the public z_{j,k}; no mask, no share

negative_vectors.scenario: BCC path: the same joint nonce (same w1) is consumed for a second partial, even relabeled under a fresh nonceID or across two committees sharing the victim.
negative_vectors.expected_outcome: ErrNonceReused (no second z-partial emitted; (c_A-c_B)*s1 system never assembled)

negative_vectors.scenario: A partial's SessionID/NonceID does not match the round.
A second partial carrying an already-seen PartyID (sub-threshold-via-duplicates vector).
A partial's PartyID is outside the signing quorum's eval-point range.
A partial's ZShare/Proof does not parse for the mode (unpackPolyVecChecked fails, no panic).
A partial's linear-sigma proof fails verification (bound to session,nonce,party,c,lambda).
An attacker stamps a victim's PartyID on a forged partial to get the victim blamed or front-run-excluded.
negative_vectors.expected_outcome: dropped pre-attribution; NO blame against the slot's honest owner; nil verifier itself REFUSED fail-closed (ErrOriginAuthRequired)
negative_vectors.code_ref: distributed_bcc.go:883 (auth gate before attribution); ErrOriginAuthRequired :813; UnauthenticatedAggregation opt-out :836

negative_vectors.scenario: A malicious member submits a VALID-sigma but WRONG-z partial (BCC), or an equivocated-at-keygen-but-self-consistent share (hyperball).
negative_vectors.expected_outcome: wrong aggregate fails FIPS-204 verify (no forgery, no leak); attribution requires BDLOP dealt-share binding -> ErrIdentifiableAbortResidual
negative_vectors.code_ref: share_commit.go:73 (ErrIdentifiableAbortResidual); shareCommitmentsFor returns nil today share_commit.go:94-96; hyperball analogue mithril_rss_hyperball.go:633-637
negative_vectors.property: robustness residual (BDLOP plug point) — flagged honestly, fail-closed-pending-review

negative_vectors.scenario: Attempt a naive additive (Pedersen/Gennaro) dealerless byte-FIPS-204 ML-DSA DKG for any committee of parties>=2 (the additive lift, NOT RSS).
negative_vectors.expected: reject (fail-closed, never fakes a key, never silently falls back to dealer/TEE)
negative_vectors.expected_outcome: ErrDealerlessByteFIPSUnreachable; computed obstruction JointS2Linf=N*eta, JointCS2Linf=N*beta > beta
negative_vectors.property: keygen obstruction (parameter-derived; RSS ia.cr/2026/013 is the NAMED ESCAPE — small s2=Sum chi_eta, not an N-way additive blow-up; Corona additionally carries dealerless safety)

cross_version_kats.note: Committed wire KATs replayed every CI run; drift from runtime output is a gate failure. These prove OLD WIRE COMPATIBILITY across versions. The v0.6.1/v0.6.2 mlwe de-dup (transcript/Shamir/SHAKE-samplers/ring routed onto luxfi/mlwe) was BYTE-PRESERVING — these KATs replay identically after the refactor.

cross_implementation.proves: shared luxfi/dkg ring reproduces pulsar's FIPS-204 t1 byte-identical to circl; A-matrix round-trips with no spurious Montgomery factor
cross_implementation.honest_note: the vss no-reconstruct group key T=A*s1+B*u (large s2=B*u) is NOT directly BCC-signable (dkg_vss_kat_test.go:30-35) — which is exactly why RSS (small s2=Sum chi_eta), not vss, is the dealerless keygen that beats the wall

proof_checklist_summary.note: Full 18-row table in DKG-PROOF-BOUNDARY.md §7. Assurance tags per PROOF-CLAIMS.md vocabulary.

rows.test: NOT PROVEN (structural facts + stock-circl-verifiability only, rows 2,4)
